hypothesis: If 3D molecule generation models are trained and evaluated with corrected aromatic valency (via accurate lookup tables or Kekulized GEOM-Drugs) and assessed using GFN2-xTB, then they will produce molecules with fewer valency violations and lower geometry/relaxation deviations than models trained/evaluated on the original dataset with MMFF94, because fixing the valency bug removes systematic aromatic-bond errors and xTB better reflects quantum-consistent geometries.
